title: The Craps Masterclass: Lowering House Edge with AI Betting Logic
excerpt: Craps is the loudest and most complex game on the floor, but it also offers one of the lowest house edges if you know which bets to ignore. Master the 'Odd' bets and AI-optimized strategies.
author: Dice Analysis Specialist
metaTitle: Craps Strategy Guide | Rules, Odds & AI Betting Patterns | Calcasi
metaDescription: Master the world's most exciting dice game. Learn where to place the most mathematically advantageous bets, which high-payout 'sucker bets' to avoid, and how AI logic maximizes your Craps success.
content:
Craps is a game where knowledge is power. To the untrained eye, the table looks like a chaotic mess of numbers and jargon. However, for an analytical player, it is a goldmine where the house edge can be driven down to almost **zero** through specific betting combinations.

This guide demystifies Craps and focuses on the high-probability bets that professional players and AI algorithms prioritize.

The Flow of the Game

Craps begins with the **Come-Out Roll**. This initial roll determines the 'Point' for the rest of the round.

Outcomes of the Come-Out Roll

  • 7 or 11: Immediate Win (for 'Pass Line' bettors).
  • 2, 3, or 12: Immediate Loss ('Craps').
  • 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, or 10: The 'Point' is established. The game continues until either the Point number is rolled again (Win) or a 7 is rolled (Loss).

The Three Golden Bets

The Pass Line

The safest and most common bet. You are betting with the shooter. It has a very low house edge of 1.41%.

Don't Pass

The opposite of the Pass Line. You are betting 'against' the table. Statistically, this is slightly *better* than the Pass Line with an edge of 1.36%.

Odds Bets (The Secret Weapon)

Once a Point is established, you can place an 'Odds' bet behind your main bet. This is the **only bet in the entire casino with zero house edge**. The casino pays true mathematical odds. Always maximize your Odds bet if your bankroll allows.

Bets to Avoid (The Sucker Bets)

Craps is filled with 'Prop Bets' that offer high payouts but have a massive house edge (up to 16%!). Stay away from these:

  • Any Craps / Any 7
  • Hardways (e.g., Hard 8)
  • Big 6 or Big 8

They are designed to tempt players with 'lottery style' wins while steadily draining their bankroll.

Applying Betting Systems to Craps

Because the Pass Line/Don't Pass odds are so stable, Craps is a perfect environment for betting progressions:

  • Martingale: Effective for recovering after a single '7-out'.
  • D'Alembert: For those seeking a more conservative, steady session.
  • Parlay: Aggressively rolling your winnings into the next Point for massive payouts.

The AI-Optimized Craps Strategy

An AI model playing Craps focuses exclusively on the math. The AI logic is as follows:

  1. **Pass Line + Max Odds:** This combination reduces the total house edge to under 0.5%.
  2. **Strict Bankroll Pacing:** Set a base unit that is 1/50th of your session bankroll.
  3. **Avoid Propositions:** Zero exceptions. The high edge on center-table bets is mathematically unacceptable for long-term profit.

FAQ

Is Craps too complicated for beginners?

The table looks complex, but the game is simple. If you only bet on the 'Pass Line,' you're playing like a pro from day one.

What is the 'Best' bet in Craps?

The 'Odds' bet. It has 0% house edge and is the best value in any online casino.

Should I ever bet 'Don't Pass'?

Mathematically, yes. It's slightly better than the Pass Line. However, in live casinos, it's often seen as 'rooting against' the other players, so be prepared for a quieter experience!

Summary

In Craps, complexity is a distraction. Ignore the noise of the PROP bets, focus on the Pass Line/Odds combination, and apply a disciplined betting system. That is how you dominate the Craps table.
